title: A Nordic-inspired sauna just opened in the White Mountains
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: White Mountain Sauna Haus opened in North Conway, N.H., offering Finnish saunas, plunge pools, and a Nordic-inspired cafe.
Co-owners Levi Lucy and Bryce Harrison transformed an 1800s barn into a sauna and wellness space without phone use inside.
Open Thursday to Monday, the sauna charges $36 per visit and welcomes guests aged 12 and older.

title: What Patriots coach Mike Vrabel said about viral photos with NFL reporter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel dismissed viral photos with NFL reporter Dianna Russini in Arizona as innocuous.
Both Vrabel and Russini stated photos lacked context, with Russini noting they were with a group of six.
The Athletic's editor defended Russini, calling the photos misleading and highlighting her professional integrity.

title: The perfect spring weekend starts with daffodils
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Eight daffodil fields across Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and Connecticut are highlighted for spring viewing.
Massachusetts state parks open for camping with information on costs, reservations, and pet policies.
Lowell church linked to Jack Kerouac, purchased by Zach Bryan, to become Jack Kerouac Center offering cultural experiences.

title: Matt Damon and Noah Kahan will join forces on ‘SNL’ in May
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon will host 'Saturday Night Live' on May 9, 2026, with Noah Kahan as musical guest.
Noah Kahan will perform on 'SNL' two weeks after releasing his album 'The Great Divide' on April 25.
Matt Damon previously hosted 'SNL' in 2002 and 2018, marking his third time as host.

title: Land court backs Somerville church’s plan for homeless shelter that raised neighborhood concerns
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A Land Court judge ruled that First Church Somerville can open a 26-bed homeless shelter in its basement.
The court's decision is based on the Dover Amendment, allowing religious projects to bypass local zoning laws.
Neighbors expressed concerns about the shelter's 'low-barrier' status, fearing increased drug activity in the area.

title: Everett moves to rein in data centers as pushback grows across Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Everett's Planning Board endorsed a zoning ordinance to limit data centers in the 100-acre Docklands Innovation District.
The proposed ordinance requires data centers in the district to be limited to 20,000 square feet or five megawatts.
Local opposition in Everett aligns with statewide concerns about data centers' environmental and resource impact.

title: South Shore med spa owner pleads guilty to administering thousands of fake Botox and other treatments
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli pleaded guilty to charges related to administering counterfeit Botox and other treatments in Boston federal court.
Fadanelli, operating under aliases, imported fake cosmetic products from China and Brazil without proper licensing.
Investigators seized counterfeit products intended for injection, and multiple complaints were reported against her practices.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about praise from Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for not making excuses and leading the team to playoff position.
Joe Mazzulla credited Celtics players for their competitive character and improvement, including Jaylen Brown's standout performance this season.
Celtics maintained the No. 2 seed in the East despite roster changes, praised by Brad Stevens for strong coaching and staff chemistry.

title: ‘That tells me a lot’: Alex Cora impressed by Trevor Story’s response to early-season adversity
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Trevor Story had a slow start, hitting .111 with a .333 OPS in his first nine games for the Red Sox.
Manager Alex Cora praised Story's determination after a 2 RBI double helped end a Red Sox 3-game losing streak.
Cora highlighted Story's proactive approach and past turnaround after a Milwaukee series as positive signs.

title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Swansea police seized five unregistered guns from a home after a second grader allegedly brought a weapon to school.
Search warrant executed led to recovery of guns found improperly stored; no valid Firearms Identification Card held by residents.
Officers plan to file weapons charges against two residents, with increased police presence expected at Mark G. Hoyle Elementary.
